intent: Classroom peer banter — the player chooses Caden's conviction (what he's 'for'). The choice sets a flag the professor and the fair echo back, and that the incident turns tragic.

n0.text: The Welcome Fair's tomorrow, Caden. You get to try every discipline before you ever choose.

n1.text: Every discipline. As if the choosing were the interesting part.

n2.text: Don't mind him. It matters. So — what are you for, Caden?

choices.label: To keep people safe. Like you.

purpose.text: "What you're for." The luxury of a boy the Road has never once stood against.

purpose_close.text: Purpose is fine. Just leave room in it for other people. Promise me that much.

protect.text: Then we're the same, you and I. Hold the line and you'll never be lost.

protect_close.text: Safe. Everyone says safe. Nobody ever asks: safe for whom?

unsure.text: Honest, at least. Most people lie about that until the day it's chosen for them.

unsure_close.text: Then stay close to me tomorrow. I'll show you what being sure looks like.
